provisions.content: These by-laws may be cited as the Muheza District Council (Development Levy) by-laws 2002 and shall be deemed to have come into operation on the first day of January, 2002.
These By-laws shall apply to the whole of the area under the jurisdiction of the Muheza District Council.

For the purpose of these By-laws: " levy " means the annual rate designated as 'Development Levy' made by the Council and payable under the provisions of these by-laws and in accordance with the Local Government Finance Act 1982. " council " means Muheza District Council [ Act No. 9 of 1982 ]

With effect from first day of January each year; every person resident within the Muheza District Council who is liable to pay any rate made by the Council shall pay to the Council a levy as shown in the First, Second and Third Schedule to these by-laws accordingly.
An employing authority shall deduct the amount of the levy due from its employees and submit the sum to the District Council treasury before the beginning of the penalty period.
It shall be the duty of a Kitongoji Chairman or Employer to ascertain that all persons liable to pay the development levy in his Kitongoji organization have paid it to the Council.
The thirtieth day of September every year, shall be the last day upon which every person liable to pay the levy shall have paid it to the Council.

Where after the thirtieth day of September each year a person liable to pay the levy has not paid he shall be liable to a penalty for the first month of equal to fifty percentum of the levy payable by him. For every month succeeding the first month of delayed payment, the penalty shall rise by ten percentum of the levy payable and the penalty due at the end of the previous month.

Where after a person liable to pay the levy has failed to comply with section-4 and 8 of these by-laws, the Council shall apprehend any of his property movable or immovable which will be disposed of by public auction after Hours of holding the property without being recovered by the owner by paying the levy penalty due. The proceeds of the sale will be used to pay the levy and penalty due plus auctioneers fees and all other expenses incurred by the Council in the process, the remaining sum, if any, shall be returned to the owner.

If after the thirtieth day of September a person liable to pay the rate has not paid it to the Council and has no property to be apprehended, shall when found guilty be liable to both, levy , the percentum penalty and fine or to imprisonment in accordance with section 21(1) of the Local Government Finance Act 1982 as amended accordingly [ Act No. 9 of 1982 ]
The rate of the levy may be changed at any time by a resolution passed at an ordinary meeting of the Council.

The Muheza District Council (Development Levy) by-laws 1997 are hereby repealed First Schedule Person holding trading licenses: License Fees Levy payable Shina Tawi Tshs. 1.000/= 5.000/= Tshs. 3.500 2,000 " 5,001/= 10,000/= " 7,000 3,500 " 10,001/= 50,000/= " 11,000 5,500 " 50,001/= 100,000/= " 15,000 7,500 " 100,000/= and above " 20,000 10,000 Second Schedule Employed persons: Income per month Levy payable Tshs.
